lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.reference: Lax & Friedrichs (1954), Comm. Pure Appl. Math. 7(1):159-193 (LF flux); src/operators/flux_1d.jl `flux_1d` ArrayOp on `CubedSphereGrid` (imperative reference). η-direction sibling of `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.json` — see that rule for the full schema-features rationale; the algebra and bindings here are the coordinate-mirror image.
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.notes: η-direction Lax-Friedrichs numerical flux at a VEdge interface on the gnomonic equidistant cubed sphere. Uses cell-center scalars `$q` from the two cells flanking the η-face (offsets -1, 0 along η at cell_center stagger) and a face-staggered Courant `$c` (offset 0 along η at v_edge stagger). The closed-form upwind-blended stencil F = (c+|c|)/2·q_south + (c-|c|)/2·q_north mirrors `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi` coefficient-for-coefficient with (xi, west, east) ↔ (eta, south, north). Cross-panel ghost extension at the southern v_edge of panel p (j=1) and the northern v_edge (j=Nc+1) is resolved by the cubed_sphere grid accessor; panel-boundary distance handling for the Courant precomputation (grid.dist_eta_bnd ↔ grid.dist_eta) is encapsulated in `_get_courant_eta` / `compute_courant_numbers_arrayop` (src/operators/flux_1d.jl). Selectors carry no `panel` field per SELECTOR_KINDS.md decision #13.
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.panel_handling: lives in the cubed_sphere grid accessor (src/grids/panel_connectivity.jl); the rule references in-panel offsets only.

metric_bindings.c: face-staggered Courant number c[p,i,j] = vel[p,i,j]·dt / dist_eta[p,i,j] at v_edge stagger; interior interfaces use grid.dist_eta, panel-boundary interfaces (j=1, j=Nc+1) use grid.dist_eta_bnd. Precomputed by the accessor and consumed as a single face-staggered binding.

flux_form.comment: F_{j+1/2} = (c+|c|)/2·q_south + (c-|c|)/2·q_north = max(c,0)·q_south + min(c,0)·q_north. `q_south` is the cell at η-offset -1 from the face, `q_north` at offset 0; `c` is the face's own Courant at v_edge offset 0.

lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.composition_hint: η-direction sibling of `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi`'s composition. Caller precomputes `c[p,i,j] = v_face[p,i,j]·dt / dist[p,i,j]` then applies this rule at every η-interface. Cell-centred tendency: T[p,i,j] = -(F[p,i,j+1]·dy[p,i,j+1] - F[p,i,j]·dy[p,i,j]) / area[p,i,j].
lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_eta.schema_notes: See `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi.json` schema_notes; symmetric story for the η axis.
limitations: Output is at v_edge stagger; the ξ-direction sibling is `lax_friedrichs_flux_cubed_sphere_xi`.
Same-direction panel-boundary flux matching (`_match_boundary_fluxes_eta!` in `src/operators/flux_1d.jl`) is a downstream conservation step; encoding the matcher in the schema is a follow-up.

Rotated-boundary flux matching (`_match_rotated_boundary_fluxes!` — applies to ξ↔η panel connections) is a coupled-axis post-processing step that cannot be expressed by either of the per-axis sibling rules in isolation; remains in the imperative wrapper until a multi-axis post-processing schema feature lands.
